verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I membrane protein (single-pass TM) with documented localization to both the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae. It belongs to the p24 transmembrane trafficking protein family, which are canonical secretory-vesicle coat proteins involved in ER-Golgi trafficking. The NCBI summary explicitly states plasma-membrane localization alongside its dominant Golgi role. While primarily a vesicular-trafficking component cycling between the ER-Golgi system and PM via secretory vesicles, it maintains a documented surface pool as part of its biological function in vesicular protein trafficking. This dual steady-state distribution (Golgi-resident with a minority PM pool from active vesicular trafficking cycling) qualifies as contextual surface accessibility.
